name: ibm-db

metadata.name: ibm-db
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Python DBI driver for DB2 (LUW, zOS, i5) and IDS

documentation: Python DBI driver for IBM Db2 for LUW, IBM Informix, IBM Db2 for iSeries(AS400) and IBM Db2 for z/OS servers

functions.name: ibmdb_tests.load_tests
documentation: Test runner for ibm_db. Normally one could use something like:
Run all in the tests directory:
Run all tests matching specified pattern or specific test:
However, for running all the tests, we need to ensure that test_000_PrepareDb
is run first to set up the database. Possibly this could be moved to a
separate task which is run by itself prior to running any tests, which

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.setUp
documentation: See the tests.py comments for this function.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.capture
documentation: This function captures the output of the current test file.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.expected_LUW
documentation: This function grabs the expected output of the current test function for LUW,
located at the bottom of the current test file.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.expected_IDS
documentation: This function grabs the expected output of the current test function for IDS,
located at the bottom of the current test file.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.expected_ZOS
documentation: This function grabs the expected output of the current test function for zOS,
located at the bottom of the current test file.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.expected_AS
documentation: This function grabs the expected output of the current test function for zOS,
located at the bottom of the current test file.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.expected_ZOS_ODBC
documentation: This function grabs the expected output of the current test function for z/OS ODBC driver,
located at the bottom of the current test file.

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.assert_expect
documentation: This function compares the captured outout with the expected out of

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.assert_expectf
documentation: This function will compare using Regular Expressions

methods.name: testfunctions.IbmDbTestFunctions.runTest
documentation: This function needs to be declared here, regardless of if there
is any body to this function

functions.name: setup._printAndExit
documentation: Print message on stdout and exit installation

functions.name: setup._compiler_include_dirs
documentation:
Return generator that spits out an include directories.
This honors env variables by way of invoking
{func}`distutils_sysconfig.customize_compiler` to get the behavior
described here:
<https://setuptools.pypa.io/en/latest/userguide/ext_modules.html#compiler-and-linker-options>
Returns:

> generator: Directory to search for header files

functions.name: setup._setWinEnv
documentation: set the win32 env, if not present

functions.name: setup._setDllPath
documentation: add add_dll_directory to ibm_db.py for python 3.8. refer https://bugs.python.org/issue36085 for details

variables.name: setup.library_so
documentation: fake, but helpful for gen_lib_options in cpython/Lib/distutils/ccompiler.py

classes.name: setup.PostInstall
documentation: Post installation - run install_name_tool on Darwin

classes.name: setup.PostBuildExt
documentation: Post build_ext - update db2 dynamic lib to use loader_path on Darwin

modules.name: ibm_db_dbi
documentation: This module implements the Python DB API Specification v2.0 for DB2 database.
| This module complies with SQLAlchemy and is                              |

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.Date
documentation:
This method can be used to get date object from integers, for
inserting it into a DATE column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.Time
documentation:
This method can be used to get time object from integers, for
inserting it into a TIME column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.Timestamp
documentation:
This method can be used to get timestamp object from integers,
for inserting it into a TIMESTAMP column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.DateFromTicks
documentation:
This method can be used to get date object from ticks seconds,
for inserting it into a DATE column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.TimeFromTicks
documentation:
This method can be used to get time object from ticks seconds,
for inserting it into a TIME column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.TimestampFromTicks
documentation:
This method can be used to get timestamp object from ticks
seconds, for inserting it into a TIMESTAMP column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.Binary
documentation:
This method can be used to store binary information, for
inserting it into a binary type column in the database.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi._get_exception
documentation: This method is used to determine the type of error that was
generated.  It takes an exception instance as an argument, and
returns exception object of the appropriate type.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi._retrieve_current_schema
documentation: This method retrieve the value of ODBC keyword CURRENTSCHEMA from DSN

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi._server_connect
documentation: This method create connection with server

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.createdb
documentation: This method creates a database by using the specified database name, code set, and mode

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.dropdb
documentation: This method drops the specified database

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.recreatedb
documentation: This method drops and then recreate the database by using the specified database name, code set, and mode

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.createdbNX
documentation: This method creates a database if it not exist by using the specified database name, code set, and mode

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.connect
documentation:
This method creates a non persistent connection to the database. It returns
a ibm_db_dbi.Connection object.

functions.name: ibm_db_dbi.pconnect
documentation:
This method creates persistent connection to the database. It returns
a ibm_db_dbi.Connection object.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.Error

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Error.__init__
documentation: This is the constructor which take one string argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Error.__str__
documentation: Converts the message to a string.

This is the base class of all other exception thrown by this
module.  It can be use to catch all exceptions with a single except
statement.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.Warning

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Warning.__init__
documentation: This is the constructor which take one string argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Warning.__str__
documentation: Converts the message to a string.

This exception is used to inform the user about important
warnings such as data truncations.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.InterfaceError
documentation:
This exception is raised when the module interface is being
used incorrectly.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.DatabaseError
documentation: This exception is raised for errors related to database.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.InternalError
documentation:
This exception is raised when internal database error occurs,
such as cursor is not valid anymore.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.OperationalError
documentation:
This exception is raised when database operation errors that are
not under the programmer control occur, such as unexpected
disconnect.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.ProgrammingError
documentation:
This exception is raised for programming errors, such as table
not found.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.IntegrityError
documentation:
This exception is thrown when errors occur when the relational
integrity of database fails, such as foreign key check fails.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.DataError
documentation:
This exception is raised when errors due to data processing,
occur, such as divide by zero.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.NotSupportedError
documentation:
This exception is thrown when a method in this module or an
database API is not supported.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.DBAPITypeObject

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.DBAPITypeObject.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for DBAPITypeObject.  It takes a tuple of
database column type as an argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.DBAPITypeObject.__cmp__
documentation:
This method checks if the string compared with is in the
tuple provided to the constructor of this object.  It takes
string as an argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.DBAPITypeObject.__eq__
documentation:
This method checks if the string compared with is in the
tuple provided to the constructor of this object.  It takes
string as an argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.DBAPITypeObject.__ne__
documentation:
This method checks if the string compared with is not in the
tuple provided to the constructor of this object.  It takes
string as an argument.

Class used for creating objects that can be used to compare
in order to determine the python type to provide in parameter
sequence argument of the execute method.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for Connection object. It takes ibm_db
connection handler as an argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.__get_dbms_name
documentation: This method is used to get the DBMS_NAME

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.__get_dbms_ver
documentation: This method is used to get the DBMS_ver

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.close
documentation:
This method closes the Database connection associated with
the Connection object.  It takes no arguments.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.commit
documentation:
This method commits the transaction associated with the
Connection object.  It takes no arguments.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.rollback
documentation:
This method rollbacks the transaction associated with the
Connection object.  It takes no arguments.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.cursor
documentation:
This method returns a Cursor object associated with the
Connection.  It takes no arguments.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.set_option
documentation:
Input: connection attribute dictionary
Return: True on success or False on failure

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.get_option
documentation:
Input: connection attribute key
Return: current setting of the resource attribute requested

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.set_fix_return_type
documentation: Sets FIX_RETURN_TYPE. Added for performance improvement

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.set_autocommit
documentation:
Input: connection attribute: true if AUTOCOMMIT ON, false otherwise (i.e. OFF)
Return: True on success or False on failure

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.set_current_schema
documentation:
Input: connection attribute dictionary
Return: True on success or False on failure

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.get_current_schema
documentation: Return: current setting of the schema attribute

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.server_info
documentation: Retrieves the IBM Data Server version for a given Connection object

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.tables
documentation:
Input: connection - ibm_db.IBM_DBConnection object
Return: sequence of table metadata dicts for the specified schema
Retrieves the tables for a specified schema (and/or given table name)

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.indexes
documentation:
Input: connection - ibm_db.IBM_DBConnection object
   Return: sequence of index metadata dicts for the specified table
Example:
   Index metadata retrieved from schema 'PYTHONIC.TEST_TABLE' table
   {
   'TABLE_SCHEM':       'PYTHONIC',              'TABLE_CAT':          None,
   'TABLE_NAME':        'ENGINE_USERS',          'PAGES':              None,
   'COLUMN_NAME':       'USER_ID'                'FILTER_CONDITION':   None,
   'INDEX_NAME':        'SQL071201150750170',    'CARDINALITY':        None,
   'ORDINAL_POSITION':   1,                      'INDEX_QUALIFIER':   'SYSIBM',
   'TYPE':               3,
   'NON_UNIQUE':         0,
   'ASC_OR_DESC':       'A'
   }
Retrieves metadata pertaining to index for specified schema (and/or table name)

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.primary_keys
documentation:
Input: connection - ibm_db.IBM_DBConnection object
   Return: sequence of PK metadata dicts for the specified table
Example:
   PK metadata retrieved from 'PYTHONIC.ORDERS' table
   {
   'TABLE_SCHEM':  'PYTHONIC',                 'TABLE_CAT': None,
   'TABLE_NAME':   'ORDERS',
   'COLUMN_NAME':  'ORDER_ID'
   'PK_NAME':      'SQL071128122038680',
   'KEY_SEQ':       1
   }
Retrieves metadata pertaining to primary keys for specified schema (and/or table name)

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.foreign_keys
documentation:
Input: connection - ibm_db.IBM_DBConnection object
   Return: sequence of FK metadata dicts for the specified table
Example:
   FK metadata retrieved from 'PYTHONIC.ENGINE_EMAIL_ADDRESSES' table
   {
   'PKTABLE_SCHEM': 'PYTHONIC',                 'PKTABLE_CAT':    None,
   'PKTABLE_NAME':  'ENGINE_USERS',             'FKTABLE_CAT':    None,
   'PKCOLUMN_NAME': 'USER_ID',                  'UPDATE_RULE':    3,
   'PK_NAME':       'SQL071205090958680',       'DELETE_RULE':    3
   'KEY_SEQ':        1,                         'DEFERRABILITY':  7,
   'FK_NAME':       'SQL071205091000160',
   'FKCOLUMN_NAME': 'REMOTE_USER_ID',
   'FKTABLE_NAME':  'ENGINE_EMAIL_ADDRESSES',
   'FKTABLE_SCHEM': 'PYTHONIC'
   }
Retrieves metadata pertaining to foreign keys for specified schema (and/or table name)

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Connection.columns
documentation:
Input: connection - ibm_db.IBM_DBConnection object
   Return: sequence of column metadata dicts for the specified schema
Example:
   Column metadata retrieved from schema 'PYTHONIC.FOO' table, column 'A'
   {
   'TABLE_NAME':        'FOO',        'NULLABLE':           1,
   'ORDINAL_POSITION':   2L,          'REMARKS':            None,
   'COLUMN_NAME':       'A',          'BUFFER_LENGTH':      30L,
   'TYPE_NAME':         'VARCHAR',    'SQL_DATETIME_SUB':   None,
   'COLUMN_DEF':         None,        'DATA_TYPE':          12,
   'IS_NULLABLE':       'YES',        'SQL_DATA_TYPE':      12,
   'COLUMN_SIZE':        30L,         'TABLE_CAT':          None,
   'CHAR_OCTET_LENGTH':  30L,         'TABLE_SCHEM':       'PYTHONIC',
   'NUM_PREC_RADIX':     None,
   'DECIMAL_DIGITS':     None
   }
Retrieves the columns for a specified schema (and/or table name and column name)

This class object represents a connection between the database
and the application.

classes.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.__get_description
documentation:
If this method has already been called, after executing a select statement,
return the stored information in the self.__description.
This method is used to get the description attribute.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.__get_rowcount
documentation: This method is used to get the rowcount attribute.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.__get_connection
documentation: This method is used to get the Connection object

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for Cursor object. It takes ibm_db connection
handler as an argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.close
documentation:
This method closes the cursor object.  After this method is
called the cursor object is no longer usable.  It takes no
arguments.
This method closes the statemente associated with the cursor object.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.callproc
documentation:
This method can be used to execute a stored procedure.
It takes the name of the stored procedure and the parameters to
the stored procedure as arguments.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._prepare_helper
documentation: Helper for preparing an SQL statement.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._set_cursor_helper
documentation: Helper for preparing an SQL statement.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._execute_helper
documentation: Helper for executing an SQL statement.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._set_rowcount
documentation: This method is used to set the rowcount after executing an SQL

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._get_last_identity_val
documentation:
The result of the IDENTITY_VAL_LOCAL function is not affected by the following:
 - A single row INSERT statement with a VALUES clause for a table without an
identity column
 - A multiple row INSERT statement with a VALUES clause
 - An INSERT statement with a fullselect
Retrieves the last generated identity value from the DB2 catalog

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.execute
documentation:
This method can be used to prepare and execute an SQL
statement.  It takes the SQL statement(operation) and a
sequence of values to substitute for the parameter markers in
the SQL statement as arguments.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.executemany
documentation:
This method can be used to prepare, and then execute an SQL
statement many times.  It takes the SQL statement(operation)
and sequence of sequence of values to substitute for the
parameter markers in the SQL statement as its argument.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._fetch_helper
documentation:
This method is a helper function for fetching fetch_size number of
rows, after executing an SQL statement which produces a result set.
It takes the number of rows to fetch as an argument.
If this is not provided it fetches all the remaining rows.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.fetchone
documentation:
This method fetches one row from the database, after
executing an SQL statement which produces a result set.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.fetchmany
documentation:
This method fetches size number of rows from the database,
after executing an SQL statement which produces a result set.
It takes the number of rows to fetch as an argument.  If this
is not provided it fetches self.arraysize number of rows.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.fetchall
documentation:
This method fetches all remaining rows from the database,
after executing an SQL statement which produces a result set.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor.nextset
documentation:
This method can be used to get the next result set after
executing a stored procedure, which produces multiple result sets.

methods.name: ibm_db_dbi.Cursor._fix_return_data_type
documentation: This method is used to convert a string representing decimal
and binary data in a row tuple fetched from the database
to decimal and binary objects, for returning it to the user.

This class represents a cursor of the connection.  It can be
used to process an SQL statement.
Defines a cursor for the driver connection
